claim: SHR-2002 is a TIGIT/PVRIG bispecific antibody engineered by fusing anti-PVRIG nanobodies to the N terminus of anti-TIGIT antibodies.
evidence: PubMed abstract for PMID 39851063 states the BsAb was engineered by fusing anti-PVRIG nanobodies to the N terminus of anti-TIGIT antibodies.

claim: The dual target rationale is TIGIT and PVRIG co-expression on activated T and NK cells and tumor immune evasion.
evidence: PubMed abstract says TIGIT and PVRIG are co-expressed on activated T and NK cells and contribute to tumor immune evasion.

claim: The BsAb blocked TIGIT/CD155 and PVRIG/CD112 ligand interactions and increased T-cell activation and NK-cell cytotoxicity.
evidence: Abstract reports effective blockade of TIGIT and PVRIG ligand binding, T-cell activation increase of 2.8-fold, and NK-cell cytotoxicity increase of 1.8-fold.
source: PubMed PMID 39851063; AACR Figshare article abstract

claim: In vivo activity was observed as monotherapy and with PD-1/PD-L1 blockade in humanized PBMC-reconstituted and transgenic mouse models.
evidence: Abstract reports potent antitumor activity as monotherapy and in combination with anti-PD-1 or anti-PD-L1 in humanized PBMC-reconstituted and transgenic mouse models.

claim: Cynomolgus monkey PK/safety was favorable with no dose-limiting toxicity after four repeated 200 mg/kg doses.
evidence: Abstract reports favorable PK profile and no dose-limiting toxicities after four repeated doses of 200 mg/kg in cynomolgus monkeys.
source: PubMed PMID 39851063; AACR Figshare supplement page

claim: PVRIG-30 is a useful local sequence-level calibration arm for the SHR-2002 mechanism, but should not be treated as our new candidate.
evidence: WO2021180205A1 Table 2 provides PVRIG-30 HCVR SEQ ID NO:3; local FASTA has this sequence and ANARCI/IMGT CDR table.
